reasoning: The initial jobless claims stood at 236,000 at the knowledge cutoff (week ending 2025-08-30). Over the preceding months, values had generally hovered in the 220k-230k range, suggesting 236k is a local peak that is likely to experience mean reversion in the short term. The FRED tool projects a predicted value of ~231.5k by September 7th and gives a 36% combined probability of exceeding 236k based on linear models and seasonal analogs. As the forecast horizon extends, uncertainty increases and the overall upwards drift in the labor market baseline comes into play (a trend of +~900 cases per month). Thus, probability approaches 50% for 6 months out (early 2026) as uncertainty compounds and the projected trend line crosses the 236k threshold. The assigned probabilities reflect this gradual convergence back up to coin-flip confidence as the time horizon grows.
